title: FillPackageJsonV2 executor
description: Generate a spec-correct package.json with modern exports map (types first, default last), sideEffects, top-level fallback fields and CDN fields (unpkg, jsdelivr).

outputPath.description: Directory where the package.json will be written (relative to workspace root).

packageVersion.description: Package version. Overrides whatever is in the project package.json.

sideEffects.description: Value for the sideEffects field. If omitted, the project package.json's value is used; if missing there, defaults to false.

topLevelFields.description: Auto-synthesize top-level main / module / types fields from the '.' export.

cdn.description: CDN script-tag entry. Set to a path string (relative to outputPath) to populate `unpkg` and `jsdelivr` fields.

populateExports.description: Controls how the `exports` map is populated. Use `'auto'` (default) to read the build manifest when present and fall back to the `_exports` template flow; `'skip'`/`false` leaves the field untouched; an object provides explicit configuration.

source.description: Where to source the entry list and output paths from. 'manifest' = read .build-manifest.json; 'exports' = use entries+templates; 'auto' picks manifest if present.

manifestFile.description: Manifest file name (relative to outputPath). Only consulted when source resolves to 'manifest'.

exports.description: Conditional export keys to emit, in spec order. 'types' must be first, 'default' must be last; the executor enforces this regardless of input order.

entries.description: Subpath entries to emit (in addition to '.'). Trailing slash means a barrel directory.

entriesTemplateProperty.description: Property name in the project package.json to read entries from when `entries` is not provided. Removed from the final output.

useFesm.description: Use the FESM flat ESM bundle for `import`/`default` conditions instead of the per-file ESM build. Only valid when there is a single '.' entry.

dcts.description: Include a `.d.cts` types entry alongside `.d.ts` for the `require` condition.
